raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need find foo in /workspace, then sort by modification date and long listing. Use find to list, then sort by -t? Actually sort by modification date: use ls -lt? But need to display long listing of the file(s). We can use find to get paths, then xargs ls -lt. But sorting by modification date across all found files. Use ls -lt $(find /workspace -name foo). But if many, use xargs. So command: find /workspace -name foo -print0 | xargs -0 ls -lt. That will list sorted by modification date descending. Output one line.
raw_output_payload.reasoning_content: Need find foo in /workspace, then sort by modification date and long listing. Use find to list, then sort by -t? Actually sort by modification date: use ls -lt? But need to display long listing of the file(s). We can use find to get paths, then xargs ls -lt. But sorting by modification date across all found files. Use ls -lt $(find /workspace -name foo). But if many, use xargs. So command: find /workspace -name foo -print0 | xargs -0 ls -lt. That will list sorted by modification date descending. Output one line.
